name: kaggle-competition-coordination
title: Kaggle Competition Coordination
description: Coordinate a team through a Kaggle competition: gather profiles (fan-out), match competitions, lead approval, repo + solution proposal, reviewer-iterate loop, submit, then admin next-steps.
preamble:
## Domain Context
A coordinator drives a team through one Kaggle competition. Members self-report profiles via child workflows; an agent matches competitions to the team; a designated lead approves one; a repo is created and an agent drafts a written solution proposal; a reviewer (picked by fit and availability, never the lead) iterates on the proposal until approving submission; the solution is submitted to Kaggle and the public score returns to the lead/administrator to decide next steps.

triggerInput.name: leadUserId
triggerInput.description: User id of the competition lead (also the administrator consulted after submission).

triggerInput.name: durationHours
triggerInput.description: Hours to wait for team members to fill in their profiles (e.g. 24).

triggerInput.name: namespace
triggerInput.description: Namespace handle to fetch team members from.

triggerInput.name: githubOwner
triggerInput.description: GitHub organization that will own the created competition repo.

steps.name: Fetch members
steps.description: Fetch organization members from the platform API as table-editor options.

steps.name: Select team members
steps.description: Coordinator selects which members take part in this competition.

config.emptyMessage: No members found in the namespace.

steps.name: Prepare roster
steps.description: Filter the selected members and compute the profile-collection deadline.

steps.name: Gather profiles
steps.description: Spawn one kaggle-collect-profile child workflow per selected member.

steps.name: Await profiles
steps.description: Wait until the profile-collection deadline passes.

steps.name: Collect profiles
steps.description: Fetch each spawned child run and extract its profile params into a team profile list.

steps.name: Fetch competitions
steps.description: Fetch active Kaggle competitions via the Kaggle API.

steps.name: Match competitions
steps.description: Rank competitions against the team profile and produce a shortlist for the lead to pick.

steps.name: Lead picks a competition
steps.description: Competition lead picks one competition from the shortlist, or sends it back, or rejects all.

steps.name: Create repository
steps.description: Create a private GitHub repo for the chosen competition under the configured org.

steps.name: Pick reviewer
steps.description: Pick a reviewer (never the lead) by expertise fit and availability.

agent.prompt:
# Select a Solution Reviewer

Read `/output/input.json`:
- `steps.collect_profiles.profiles[]` - team profiles incl. `availability` (high/medium/low).
- `steps.lead_pick` - the chosen competition (`ref`, `title`, `category`, `fitRationale`).
- `leadUserId` - the competition lead; the reviewer MUST NOT be this person.

## Task
Pick exactly one team member to review the solution. Weigh (a) expertise fit for this competition's domain (from strengths/interests) and (b) current availability - prefer `high`, avoid `low` (overloaded). Exclude `leadUserId`. Explain the choice.

## OUTPUT CONTRACT (MANDATORY)
1) Write result JSON to `/output/result.json`.
2) Your FINAL message must be ONLY: {"output_file": "/output/result.json", "summary": "..."}

result.json shape:
{ "reviewerUserId": "<userId, never the lead>", "reviewerName": "<name>", "rationale": "<expertise + availability reasoning>" }

steps.name: Propose solution
steps.description: Draft a written solution proposal; on revise loops, incorporate reviewer feedback.

agent.prompt:
# Propose a Solution Approach

Read `/output/input.json`:
- `steps.lead_pick` - chosen competition (`ref`, `title`, `category`, `fitRationale`).
- `steps.collect_profiles.profiles[]` - team strengths, to assign work to specific members.
- `steps.create_repo.body.json.html_url` - the created repo URL (if present).
- `steps.review_solution.feedback` - reviewer feedback from a prior iteration (PRESENT ONLY on revise loops). If present, address every point.

## Task
Write a concrete, written solution PROPOSAL (not code): approach, model choice, data strategy, evaluation metric aligned to the competition, and a task split mapping work to specific team members by strength. List key risks. On a revise loop, revise the prior proposal to address the reviewer feedback.

## OUTPUT CONTRACT (MANDATORY)
1) Write result JSON to `/output/result.json`.
2) Your FINAL message must be ONLY: {"output_file": "/output/result.json", "summary": "..."}

result.json shape:
{
  "proposal": {
    "approach": "...",
    "modelChoice": "...",
    "dataStrategy": "...",
    "evalMetric": "...",
    "taskSplit": [ { "member": "<name or userId>", "task": "..." } ],
    "risks": ["..."]
  },
  "summary": "..."
}

steps.name: Review solution
steps.description: Selected reviewer approves the proposal for submission, or requests changes.

params.name: feedback
params.description: What to change in the proposal (required when requesting changes).

steps.name: Provide submission file
steps.description: Provide a direct download URL to the predictions file the team produced in the repo.

params.name: submissionFileUrl
params.description: Direct download URL to the predictions CSV (raw file URL).

steps.name: Submit to Kaggle
steps.description: Download the predictions file, submit to Kaggle, and read back the public score.

steps.name: Administrator next steps
steps.description: Show the Kaggle public score to the administrator and decide whether to iterate, finish, or abandon.

params.name: notes
params.description: Notes on the result and next steps.
